title: How could the SDSS have predicted Tabris
display_title: How could the SDSS have predicted Tabris
wikitext:
[]: How could the SDSS have predicted Tabris?             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 14, 2010 6:32 pm This just occurred to me, the SDSS predict the number of Angels and name them, right? That would include Tabris as the last Angel... which at first makes no sense because Tabris is an artificial creation. But what if Tabris, in some form, was inevitable no matter what Seele had done? In other words, what if the end of Adam's natural cycle, after producing the other Angels and giving them souls, is to recycle herself into Tabris? Alternatively, what if Adam simply never finished creating Tabris (interrupted by Lilith's arrival), and so Seele created Kaworu to fill that role so that they could fulfill the prophecy? These can also be combined... Perhaps Adam's natural cycle is that the last Angel she makes is the body she then puts her own soul into, whereafter her role is over and her 15 "children" (including "herself") then rule the planet.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 14, 2010 6:39 pm This just occurred to me, the SDSS predict the number of Angels and name them, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 14, 2010 7:02 pm Monk Ed wrote: That would include Tabris as the last Angel...

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Mon Jun 14, 2010 7:45 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Adam was never meant to have a "set" number of children or an "end to her lifecycle" like you seem to think. If not for Lilith's presence fucking things up, the planet would be FULL of Angels, not just 15.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 14, 2010 8:35 pm Yea, but only the most retarded godlike civilization in the multiverse would deliberately make a species that's totally asexual and contingent of the existence of a single mother that's prone to exploding if you mess with it. the way that Seele releases Tabris at the right moment instead of, say, during the long month when no Angels appeared suggests that they're numbered, and the CI says that the generally recognized info is that the times of the Angel appearances are recorded there. The names also have to come from somewhere, and the CI states that 'inconsequential' parts of the DSS (our parts, presumably) were released. They had a period to count the eggs before Second Impact actually happened. Ample time to understand how many Angels there were, and give them names from human religion. SEELE gives no meaningful indication that they're actually able to predict when Angels show up, only how many there were going to be.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 14, 2010 8:45 pm Uh, they DID make the Angels contingent on the existence of a single mother. They didn't want seeds to mix, so they put in safeties. Meanwhile, Seele REPEATEDLY refers to a schedule (yes, they fall off schedule, but it clearly worked for Angels 3-10), and, as I just realized, there's this unfortunate line to my own theory, not just your own: Man D: "That leaves only one Angel described in Seele's Dead Sea Scrolls." Man D is a Seele participant, for the record. This right here says clearly that the Angels are DESCRIBED in the scrolls. That kind of throws off my stuff with Tabris, but it jettisons your thoughts completely. Now, this leaves great holes with Tabris, so... I guess I'm gonna have to pray that Reichu finds this thread, since I think she's done stuff similar to this and might have more info on hand than I do.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 14, 2010 8:53 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Yea, but only the most retarded godlike civilization in the multiverse would deliberately make a species that's totally asexual and contingent of the existence of a single mother that's prone to exploding if you mess with it.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 14, 2010 9:46 pm Legendary wrote: I guess I'm gonna have to pray that Reichu finds this thread, since I think she's done stuff similar to this and might have more info on hand than I do.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 14, 2010 9:48 pm To explain how "Seele's Dead Sea Scrolls" predict 15 children of Adam even though Tabris is supposed to be a fluke.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 14, 2010 10:32 pm Legendary wrote: To explain how "Seele's Dead Sea Scrolls" predict 15 children of Adam even though Tabris is supposed to be a fluke.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Mon Jun 14, 2010 10:39 pm Thanks for the wankery. I like C the best, and thusly declare it canonical! BTW, with "prophecy", I always assumed it was that the fanatics of Seele took it upon themselves to believe it was a prophecy, not an instruction manual, despite the realities of the situation.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Jun 14, 2010 11:36 pm Reichu wrote: (The "instruction manual" part is okay. The prophetic bullshit, not so much.)

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Tue Jun 15, 2010 3:24 am Monk Ed wrote: Hm... Gendo and Fuyutsuki have both apparently read the Scrolls, so if the Scrolls gave such instructions they'd have known what Seele was up to.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Tue Jun 15, 2010 3:33 am Jinroh wrote: In rebuild they say the scrolls are incomplete. Is it the case in Evangelion too? I don't remember.

[]:             Quote Post by carla » Tue Jun 15, 2010 9:06 am Monk Ed wrote: Could be they applied Bible Code logic to it, got lucky, etc, and decided after the fact that It Was All Foretold.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jun 16, 2010 12:11 pm Split out the Angel / Adam-based ecosystem tangent, since it was looking inevitable. Bits of an Aura post that belong here: _____________________________________ AuraTwilight Tue Jun 15, 2010 3:32 pm Legendary wrote: Meanwhile, Seele REPEATEDLY refers to a schedule (yes, they fall off schedule, but it clearly worked for Angels 3-10), and, as I just realized, there's this unfortunate line to my own theory, not just your own: Man D: "That leaves only one Angel described in Seele's Dead Sea Scrolls." Man D is a Seele participant, for the record. This right here says clearly that the Angels are DESCRIBED in the scrolls. That kind of throws off my stuff with Tabris, but it jettisons your thoughts completely.

[]: Re: How could the SDSS have predicted Tabris?             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Wed Jun 16, 2010 1:12 pm Monk Ed wrote: This just occurred to me, the SDSS predict the number of Angels and name them, right? That would include Tabris as the last Angel... which at first makes no sense because Tabris is an artificial creation. But what if Tabris, in some form, was inevitable no matter what Seele had done? In other words, what if the end of Adam's natural cycle, after producing the other Angels and giving them souls, is to recycle herself into Tabris? Alternatively, what if Adam simply never finished creating Tabris (interrupted by Lilith's arrival), and so Seele created Kaworu to fill that role so that they could fulfill the prophecy? These can also be combined... Perhaps Adam's natural cycle is that the last Angel she makes is the body she then puts her own soul into, whereafter her role is over and her 15 "children" (including "herself") then rule the planet.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sun Jun 20, 2010 7:13 pm I think the biggest issue with the SDSS is the assumption that they are accurate. Considering: They are of the Dead Sea Scrolls found in a cave...literal scrolls that tend to break down over time and only survived to modern day because someone took measures to protect them Why would the FAR include instrucion that are liable to become dust before intelligent life could make use of them? I suspect, and this is mostly conjecture, that the SDDS were the creation of another human, presumably who found the original writing of the FAR (further conjecture would suggest somewhere in or around either the Black Moon or White Moon), and was somehow able to translate them (how one could translate a completely alien language without a key is a big issue, but one that can be overlooked for the sake of storytelling). When translating, this person comes across many strange things 'complete beings of solitude that can manipluate the space around them', 'the light of the soul' and other such stuff, and comes to believe that this must surely be divine scriptures. With a religeous predispotition, the texts are translated and the scrolls are created. Naturally there will be a few things simply beyond comprehension, so 'he' uses his own religeous background to fill in the gaps, or to try and make some parts make sense, hence the references to specific angels, the fruit of knowledge, and other biblical exerpts. maybe SEELE managed to find the original source of the texts, maybe not, maybe they can't translate it temselve because they lack the plot device the author used to translate. This is all just massive conjecture, but you see my point- the SDSS are not neccessarily infallible or even 100% accurate in the information they hold, and are probably second hand information at best.

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Jun 20, 2010 10:25 pm These weren't the regular Dead Sea Scrolls. These were the SECRET Dead Sea Scrolls. That right there makes them okay and 100% accurate.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Jun 21, 2010 2:07 am Except no, it does not. It simply means no one incorporated "By the way Mommy menstruated us all out of her tang-vag" into religious canon.

 way that Seele releases Tabris at the right moment instead of, say, during the long month when no Angels appeared suggests that they're numbered, and the CI says that the generally recognized info is that the times of the Angel appearances are recorded there. The names also have to come from somewhere, and the CI states that 'inconsequential' parts of the DSS (our parts, presumably) were released. SS is the assumption that they are accurate. I suspect, and this is mostly conjecture, that the SDDS were the creation of another human, presumably who found the original writing of the FAR (further conjecture would suggest somewhere in or around either the Black Moon or White Moon), and was somehow able to translate them (how one could translate a completely alien language without a key is a big issue, but one that can be overlooked for the sake of storytelling). This is all just massive conjecture, but you see my point- the SDSS are not neccessarily infallible or even 100% accurate in the information they hold, and are probably second hand information at best.
